summary: Decompiled and typed a substantial slice of the lb audio AX code plus two lbbgflash routines. The PR body states that lbbgflash.c and lbaudio_ax.c match, while the decomp.dev report specifically records 4 new 100% matches and 10 substantial unmatched-function improvements, raising matched code by 664 bytes. The main value was replacing multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders and raw user_data/int indexing with typed prototypes, a lbAudioAx_UserData layout, static externs, and concrete HSD_GObj/audio object lifecycle logic.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; added many concrete implementations for audio sorting, panning/interpolation, GObj sound creation/freeing, voice keyoff, SFX-bank memory accounting, and stage/category mask helpers.

Converted several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to typed prototypes, including fn_80023254, lbAudioAx_8002392C, lbAudioAx_80024F08, fn_80025FAC, fn_800262A0, lbAudioAx_800264E4, lbAudioAx_80026510, fn_800267B0, fn_800268B4, lbAudioAx_8002835C, lbAudioAx_80028B6C, and lbAudioAx_80028B90.
Added lbAudioAx_UserData layout, offsets_arr_803BC4E4, and audio globals such as lbl_804337C4, lbl_804338A4, lbl_80433B44, lbl_804D6438, lbl_804D6448, lbl_804D644C, and lbl_804D6450.
Added fn_800204C8 and fn_80021C1C implementations for background flash state update and ColorOverlay handling.
Prototype cleanup for menu functions, replacing several UNK signatures with void/typed signatures.
Typed mnVibration_80249174 as void mnVibration_80249174(int arg0).
New static header with scene/color/static float externs for vi0801.
New static header with SceneDesc, archive, JObj, Vec3, and static float externs for vi1201v2.
New static header with JObj, void** global, and static float/f64 externs for vi1202.

what_changed: Implemented fn_80023254 in lbaudio_ax.c, including a 0x38-entry local array, lbl_80433B44 ordering table setup, comparisons against s32_arr_803BB5D0 and offsets_arr_803BC4E4, and an explicitly unrolled/backward shift insertion loop.

Implemented fn_800251EC as a GObj audio panning updater: it reads lbAudioAx_UserData, queries Camera_800307D0 for left/center/right camera bounds, obtains entity position through ftLib_80086644 for type 4 or it_8026B294 for type 6, and stores a computed pan into ud->x2C.pan.
Implemented pan interpolation callbacks fn_80025B44 and fn_80025CBC. Both use current_frame/end_frame and pan_left/pan_right, clamp float results to 0..127, and write either result or 0x7F - result.
Implemented fn_80025E38 to interpolate lbAudioAx_UserData x20 between start_val and end_val over current_frame/end_frame, with a fallback setting x20 to 0x7F after the end frame.

Implemented lbAudioAx_800263E8, creating an HSD_GObj with GObj_Create, allocating audio userdata via HSD_ObjAlloc, initializing userdata with GObj_InitUserData, registering fn_800262A0 with HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and passing a stack SoundParams struct into fn_80025FAC.
Implemented lbAudioAx_800264E4 to return an inner userdata voice_id or -1 when missing, though the signature is bool despite returning -1/voice_id values in the diff.

Implemented lbAudioAx_80026510 to walk HSD_GObj_Entities[0x3E], key off any matching audio voice with AXDriverKeyOff, unlink matching gobjs with HSD_GObjPLink_80390228, and return whether anything was removed.
Refined lbAudioAx_800265C4 by replacing raw int* user_data indexing and M2C_FIELD access with lbAudioAx_UserData fields and ((HSD_GObj**) HSD_GObj_Entities)[0x3E].

Implemented fn_800267B0 and fn_800268B4 for audio bank/SFX resource accounting, including counters lbl_804D6448/lbl_804D644C/lbl_804D6450, arrays at offsets from lbl_80433710, HSD_Synth_80388E08, HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflag, and HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync.

Implemented lbAudioAx_80026F2C and lbAudioAx_8002702C using u64 masks built from flag bits and constants such as 0x0048000000000003ULL, 0x3C, 0x00800003FFFFFFC0ULL, 0x0023FFFC00000000ULL, and 0x0014000000000000ULL to update lbl_804337C4 entries.
Implemented lbbgflash fn_800204C8 as a state-driven background flash update and fn_80021C1C as a wrapper that applies a ColorOverlay from a gobj's user_data via lb_80014498.
Added new static headers for vi0801, vi1201v2, and vi1202 with include guards named after GALE01 addresses and with local static float/f64 declarations plus extern scene/object globals.
Updated several headers to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete void, bool, pointer, and argument types.
smart_moves: Introduced lbAudioAx_UserData in lbaudio_ax.static.h before using it throughout lbaudio_ax.c, which made later functions readable and allowed raw user_data[2]/user_data[12] access to become entity and voice_id field references.
Kept match-oriented control flow with gotos, explicit labels, and unusual return paths instead of over-cleaning the C; this likely helped preserve codegen for functions reported near or at 100%.
Used a local SoundParams stack struct in lbAudioAx_800263E8 to document the call packet passed into fn_80025FAC while still preserving the original argument packing order.

Replaced M2C_FIELD(HSD_GObj_Entities, HSD_GObj**, 0xF8) with the clearer equivalent ((HSD_GObj**) HSD_GObj_Entities)[0x3E], improving readability without changing the underlying global entity-list indexing.
Added targeted baselib and melee includes, including gobjproc, gobjuserdata, camera, ftlib, and it_26B1, so newly typed calls use real declarations rather than placeholders.
Created static headers for vi files rather than polluting public headers with private globals, following the project's split between public APIs and translation-unit/static symbol knowledge.
Preserved explicit float casts and clamp ranges in the pan interpolation functions, important for matching PowerPC floating-point/conversion behavior.
decomp_lessons: For GObj-heavy library code, recover the userdata struct early; even a partially named layout with padding fields can unlock multiple later functions and remove brittle int-pointer indexing.
When a decompiled routine is close to matching but control flow is branchy, labels and gotos are acceptable matching tools; do not prematurely refactor into idiomatic C if branch layout matters.
Audio and visual helper modules often need cross-subsystem includes for entity position and camera data. In this PR, audio panning depended on Camera_800307D0, ftLib_80086644, and it_8026B294.
Static/private headers are useful for declaring file-local/static data arrays and globals required by matching work without implying a public API.
u64 bitmask helpers should retain exact constants and accumulation style when matching; even seemingly equivalent bitwise expressions may alter codegen.
Object lifecycle routines should type all three pieces together: HSD_GObj creation/linking, HSD_ObjAlloc userdata allocation/freeing, and audio voice keyoff/unlink cleanup.
Bot reports can distinguish full matches from near matches; a PR body saying files match may be broader/less precise than decomp.dev's per-function status.
naming_conventions: Function names remained address-based where no semantic name was established, e.g. fn_80023254, fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_800267B0, and lbAudioAx_8002702C.
Subsystem-prefixed known functions used lbAudioAx_ and lbBgFlash_ prefixes for library audio and background flash routines.
Recovered struct name lbAudioAx_UserData follows module prefix plus purpose.
The stack helper typedef SoundParams was named semantically but its fields mostly retained offset-like names such as x4, xC, x10, x20 where meaning was not proven.
Unknown struct fields retained offset names and padding arrays, e.g. x0, xC, x20, x2C, x3C, preserving evidence-grounded naming.
Private/static header include guards used GALE01 address-style names, e.g. __GALE01_31EFE4, __GALE01_3204C0, and __GALE01_321014.
Static/global arrays retained label/address names where semantic names were uncertain, such as lbl_804337C4, lbl_804338A4, lbl_80433B44, and offsets_arr_803BC4E4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit goto labels such as ret_true, set_flag, check_flag, store_pan, end, set_7f, return_gobj, return_null, loop_body, and loop_check to preserve branch structure.
Used manual loop unrolling in fn_80023254 for initializing and shifting 0x38 table entries, including an 8-at-a-time backward copy followed by a remainder loop.
Preserved direct casts and raw memory views where needed, such as type = *(u16*) entity and base = (char*) &lbl_80433710.
Kept PAD_STACK(16) in lbAudioAx_800265C4, indicating stack-shape preservation remained necessary for matching.
Used explicit f32/f64 temporaries and casts in panning/interpolation code to control floating-point operations and integer conversion order.
Represented HSD_GObj_Entities access as an indexed HSD_GObj** array at index 0x3E, matching the former 0xF8 offset.
Built 64-bit masks with addition and shifts, then iterated 0x37 entries while shifting the mask one bit per entry.
Left several functions as /// # placeholders where not yet matched, showing a staged matching approach rather than forcing speculative implementations.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code improved by 664 bytes to 48.56% (+0.02%).
4 new 100% matches were reported: lbAudioAx_8002702C (+316 bytes), fn_800267B0 (+260 bytes), lbbgflash fn_80021C1C (+44 bytes), and lbAudioAx_800264E4 (+44 bytes).

10 unmatched-function improvements were reported, including fn_800251EC to 97.02%, fn_80025CBC to 98.32%, fn_80025B44 to 98.30%, fn_80025E38 to 97.85%, lbbgflash fn_800204C8 to 96.96%, lbAudioAx_800263E8 to 99.92%, and lbAudioAx_80026510 to 99.60%.
One small .sdata2 regression was reported: -1 byte, 70.59% to 69.44%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_80025CBC, fn_80025E38, lbAudioAx_800263E8, lbAudioAx_80026510, lbAudioAx_80026F2C, or fn_800268B4.
Look for subsequent renames of lbAudioAx_UserData fields x20, x2C, start_val, end_val, pan_left, pan_right, current_frame, and end_frame.
Check whether lbAudioAx_800264E4's bool return type was later corrected to an integer type, since this diff returns -1 and voice_id values.
Search for PRs that use or modify offsets_arr_803BC4E4, lbl_804337C4, lbl_804338A4, lbl_80433B44, lbl_804D6448, lbl_804D644C, and lbl_804D6450.
Review later lbbgflash work around BgFlashData2, fn_800204C8, fn_80021C1C, and ColorOverlay to see if field names were recovered.
Search for static header conventions in vi0801.static.h, vi1201v2.static.h, and vi1202.static.h to verify whether GALE01-address include guards remained accepted style.
